gate_question: Does this exact current protocol, with current live state, allow an unprivileged attacker to extract funds or rewards beyond entitlement?

candidate.title_or_claim: TWAP manipulation sandwich on ETH→TitanX leg of swapETHForTorusAndBurn
candidate.claimed_root_cause: getTitanXQuoteForETH uses a 15-minute TWAP (shortened by oldestObservation fallback) and _swapETHForTitanX applies only a 10% slippage tolerance on top of that potentially-deflated quote, leaving amountOutMinimum well below fair market if the TWAP is manipulated
candidate.claimed_impact: Attacker sandwiches the protocol's exactInput swap, extracting value from the per-interval ETH allocation at protocol expense

live_preconditions.name: DAILY_ALLOCATION_ETH_BURNING (per-interval ETH allocation)
live_preconditions.observed_value: 500 (units: live context integer; ~3.47 ETH/interval at 144 intervals/day if in ETH)

live_preconditions.name: TITAN_X_ETH_POOL observation cardinality (determines effective TWAP window)
TITAN_X_ETH_POOL liquidity depth (determines TWAP manipulation cost)

source_code_basis.why_it_matters: Reads TWAP from TITAN_X_ETH_POOL with up to 900s window; oldestObservation fallback at L1150-1152 can shorten the window if pool cardinality is low, making manipulation cheaper
Sets amountOutMinimum = TWAP_quote * 90/100 at L1102-1103; if TWAP is deflated by X%, effective floor is (1-X%)*90% of fair value
Permissionless external entry point (nonReentrant, burnETHIntervalUpdate modifier only); attacker can time the call after TWAP manipulation
INTERVAL_TIME=10 minutes, INTERVALS_PER_DAY=144, POOL_FEE=10000 (1%); confirms per-interval ETH size and pool identity

attacker_path.actor: any unprivileged EOA or contract with sufficient capital
attacker_inputs: Sustained large buy-side swaps on TITAN_X_ETH_POOL over >=secondsAgo seconds to shift tick cumulative upward (fewer TitanX per ETH in TWAP)
Front-run buy of TitanX on TITAN_X_ETH_POOL immediately before swapETHForTorusAndBurn
Back-run sell of TitanX immediately after swapETHForTorusAndBurn executes
call_sequence: 1. Attacker executes repeated large TitanX buys on TITAN_X_ETH_POOL across multiple blocks spanning >=secondsAgo seconds (vm.warp+vm.roll in test), deflating the TWAP quote
3. adjustedTitanXAmount = deflatedQuote * 90 / 100 — amountOutMinimum is set well below fair market
4. Attacker front-runs swapETHForTorusAndBurn with a large TitanX buy, pushing spot price further above TWAP
5. Protocol's exactInput executes at inflated spot; low amountOutMinimum does not revert
6. Attacker back-runs by selling TitanX, capturing the spread
state_before: timeElapsed > INTERVAL_TIME (10 minutes since last burn)
TITAN_X_ETH_POOL TWAP at fair market tick
state_after: Protocol received fewer TitanX than fair market value for its ETH
Attacker net positive after gas and manipulation cost (to be verified by fork test)

extraction_analysis.asset_or_reward: ETH held by TorusBuyAndProcess contract (per-interval allocation from totalETHBurn)
extraction_analysis.attacker_gain: Sandwich profit: difference between TitanX bought at pre-manipulation price and sold at post-swap price, minus manipulation cost
extraction_analysis.victim_or_protocol_loss: Protocol receives fewer TitanX per ETH than fair market; fewer Torus tokens are burned per interval, diluting the burn mechanism
extraction_analysis.why_gain_exceeds_entitlement: Attacker is not a party to the protocol's burn flow; any value extracted from the swap spread comes entirely at the protocol's expense and is not an entitled fee or reward

setup: Identify TITAN_X_ETH_POOL (0xc45A81BC23A64eA556ab4CdF08A86B61cdcEEA8b) observation cardinality via slot0()
Warp time so burnETHIntervalUpdate creates a new interval with amountAllocated > 0
Execute repeated large TitanX buys on TITAN_X_ETH_POOL across vm.warp(+600s)+vm.roll(+1) iterations spanning >=secondsAgo seconds to shift tick cumulative
Record post-manipulation getTitanXQuoteForETH(ethToSwapAndBurn) as deflatedQuote; assert deflatedQuote < fairQuote
expected_assertions: assertLt(titanXReceivedByProtocol, fairQuote * 90 / 100) // protocol got <90% of fair value
assertGt(attackerTitanXProceeds - attackerTitanXSpent - manipulationCostInTitanX, 0) // net attacker profit
local_proof_required.reject_if_assertion_fails: If attackerProfit <= 0 after accounting for all swap fees (1% pool fee on each leg) and the cost of sustained TWAP manipulation on TITAN_X_ETH_POOL, the finding is not economically viable and must be REJECTED. The per-interval ETH amount (~3.47 ETH at current DAILY_ALLOCATION_ETH_BURNING=500 with 144 intervals/day) may be too small to cover manipulation cost on a high-liquidity pool.
